subject: DevOps
title: Automation Over Manual Work
description: A micro-lesson that makes Automation Over Manual Work usable.

hook.text: A developer SSH'd into a server and typed commands to deploy. It worked — until someone mistyped.

buildup.text: Manual steps are slow, error-prone, and impossible to repeat exactly the same way twice.

discovery.text: Automation scripts do the same thing every time. No typos, no forgotten steps, no 3 AM mistakes.

twist.text: Automation isn't just faster — it's documentation. The script is the truth of how things get done.

climax.text: DevOps automates builds, tests, deploys, and monitoring. If a human does it twice, automate it.

punchline.text: Automate everything repeatable. Free humans for thinking.

quiz.question: Why is automation considered documentation in DevOps?
options: The script defines exactly how a process works
